mappings.rationaleAI: Associative theories of classical and instrumental conditioning directly address modern learning theories (A.1). Pavlovian and operant conditioning address major issues in learning theories (A.2).
Encoding, storage, and retrieval of memories directly address retention and forgetting (E). False memories and pathological disorders address abnormalities of memory (F).
Reasoning, problem solving, and dual-process theory directly address the psychology of higher thought processes (G.1).
The nature of thought addresses higher thought processes (G.1). Thought-language relationship connects to G.2. Meaning and conceptualization relate to G.3.
Visual perception of lightness, colour, form, depth, and motion directly addresses perceptual learning (C).
Neural basis of motor action addresses psychomotor learning (B). Perception neuroscience addresses perceptual learning (C). Memory addresses retention (E). Reasoning addresses higher thought (G.1).
Motor control addresses psychomotor learning (B). Sensory processing relates to perceptual learning (C). Memory addresses retention (E).
Cognitive development addresses learning theories (A) and concept formation (D). Memory formation addresses retention (E). Language acquisition relates to language in higher thought (G.2).
Conditional reflexes as foundational learning theory address modern learning theories (A.1). Experimental techniques demonstrate transfer of training (A.3).
Scientific study of language provides understanding of the role of language in higher thought processes (G.2).
Linguistic diversity and typological features address language structures in higher thought (G.2).
Language processing addresses language in higher thought (G.2). Storage and retrieval relate to meaning (G.3).

mappings.vsiAuthor: Emma Borg and Sarah A. Fisher
mappings.rationaleAI: Philosophy of meaning covering word meaning and reference directly addresses meaning (G.3).
The hard problem of consciousness and attention mechanisms address higher thought processes (G.1).
Broad cognitive psychology coverage provides context for learning theories (A), perceptual learning (C), conceptual learning (D), memory (E), and higher thought (G).
Tension between instruction and progressive freedom addresses issues in learning theories (A.2). Pedagogy relates to transfer of training (A.3).
Neurodegenerative cognitive decline directly addresses abnormalities of memory including amnesia (F).
Reading disorder addresses perceptual learning (C) in visual text processing and conceptual learning (D) in language decoding.
Creative expression and problem solving address higher thought processes (G.1).
Imagination as cognitive power addresses conceptual learning (D) and higher thought processes (G.1).
Interpretation and meaning construction through language directly addresses meaning (G.3).
Language games, rule-following, and the private language argument address language in higher thought (G.2) and meaning (G.3).
AI for understanding learning addresses modern learning theories (A.1). Intelligence and understanding questions address higher thought (G.1).
